title: Block splitting alters side effects
body:
Made up example to demonsrate:
```
always_comb begin
  x = 0;
  y = z;
end
```

V3Split will split this into 2 blocks, and the one that sets `x` is then subsequently turned into an initial block, as it assigns a constant. The line coverage for `x = 0` will therefore be 1, and for `y = z` it will be some high number. User might expect them to be the same.

Raising this because I'm working on some transforms that will enable more block splitting, and as a result will alter the coverage metrics in some non-intuitive way.

What should be our policy on this? Happy to to have non-intuitive counts? Should we do what the user might expect? If so need to teach V3Split to preserve the implicit sensitivity information of combinational blocks.
